This stripper deck is exactly what I expected and is easy to use. The quality is also excellent. A great deck for beginning magicians!

This is not a stripper deck. Why do businesses think they can just pull this @%#$ constantly?
So imagine this: You're in front of an audience. You've been using your trusty 22-year old deck and you want to offer authenticity. So you pull out a new-in-wrapping deck of bicycle cards and you have the audience examine them.
"They're regular playing cards!" they'll say (THAT'S BECAUSE THEY ARE, F$@* YOU ROCK RIDGE MAGIC)
You nod your head, not knowing how right they are, until you go to strip a card....

I ended up not being able to thumb the first card put into the deck. So I asked for a mulligan. Couldn't strip the second card, either. SO, I told the audience I had done a poor job and I am a miserable magician.

THANKS TO THIS BEING A NEW DECK, while I apologized I was able to notice the visual grain of the cards going the wrong way. I carefully pulled them out while I slumped over the deck, sweating. Again, THIS IS NOT A STRIPPER DECK.

I managed to pull the first "picked" card out of the deck and said "see? I didn't get it right." Someone in the audience gasped, realizing it was the first card. I then offered another "wrong" card - the second card that had been pulled. So I got away with it.

Why? Because I'm magic. These cards are not.

First, to qualify myself, I am a professional magician, and have been studying magic for almost 50 years. These Stripper decks are outstanding quality, and I use them in my shows. Both long edges are trimmed, unlike the cheaper decks that only trim one edge. This makes it harder to see the trimming. Also, these are not factory seconds that we so often see sold at other places. These are made by the USPC company specifically for Magic Makers and are without a doubt the best stripper decks you can find. These are the only stripper decks that I use.
The orders are always fast, and I have yet to have any mishaps from them.

The Deck I got was indeed a sealed USPC deck, but it was just a regular deck and not a stripper deck. I even put the cards on a level surface on their side with many turned around and you could not see the slightest difference in height.

I purchased this for my 6 and 8 year old with intentions of filling it with cute valentine's trinkets. I decided to test it out first and glad I did so that I can keep their expectations in check. I have literally been trying for 45 minutes, using different kinds of toys, candies, etc, to get a prize with NO luck. The claw has absolutely zero strength and the space inside is so small there is not enough room for the claw to even properly get a good angle on things. Disappointing.

Within 2 hours of opening this toy the forward and backward controls stopped working. Luckily my husband has the tools and know how to fix it. But come one. Both wires tore off and we had to take the whole thing apart to fix it. Not worth the money.
Batteries don't last long----broken within  a month

Great concept- not worth the money.

It's a super cool idea but when I received it, the claw wouldn't open or close. I have to send back for a replacement because you can't buy replacement parts anywhere. The music is pretty loud and can't be lowered or turned off which is kinda annoying (It plays the famous carnival song) so now it's stuck in my head. Lol Good idea but quality control is lacking hardcore after reading reviews on how many other ppl have an issue with either knobs not working or the claw doesn't work.
It's adorable and cute but like any claw machine, hard....if not impossible sometimes to pick up the candy. Nephew liked it so that's a good thing. Wouldn't recommend it tho. Music is annoying.
Item is fun, but it does not pickup items very well and lost its luster after only a few days. If it worked better I feel they would enjoy it more. I'll take it out of the closet in a few months.

I was a little wary of this product because of all the negative reviews, but I decided to take a risk. Amazon is really good with returns if it didn't work, anyway! We were one of the lucky ones, though, because ours has worked just fine every day since Christmas (so about 2 months.) We purchased it as an added incentive for our kids to make "good choices" throughout the day. At the end of the day, if they've done well, they'll get a tootsie roll from the claw machine.

Admittedly, the music is a little loud, but I put some packing tape over the speaker on the bottom of the machine and it is much more tolerable. My kids like that it gets faster at the end and even like the funny sound it makes when they run out of time. I can see that it would probably break pretty easily if a child (or adult) was rough with the controls. They don't really feel very sturdy, but as long as whoever is playing it is careful, it should be fine.

I'd recommend this toy, as long as you're prepared to return it, just in case!
My grandsons loved this and played with it all the time. Even had friends playing and putting in real money.

This toy is just as FUN and just as FRUSTRATING as the real Mccoy!  I guess if it was too easy it wouldn't be as fun. We got this toy originally for Christmas and ordered it online, it came broken (idea mentioned below **). The claw plastic was cracked. It was EASILY returned to a local store (toys-r-us) where we got another one. My 9 year old had been dying to get this TOY for the last 2 years and after getting it set up, played it with it for days in a row literally getting the toys/candy we got for it and putting it back in over and over.

A few reviews mentioned the quality but I guess knowing it was called the "TOY Candy Claw Machine" we were expecting a TOY, not a metal, highly durable item. I have to say that the quality so far has been great. Yes the items are plastic but what isn't these days? And it has stood up to two 9 & 10 year old boys using it consistently with no issues.

Two things I HIGHLY recommend if you plan to use it as a gift or party toy:

1)Buy the candy/toys you want to use ahead of time and try them out first. Mini air-head candy is SUPER hard to grab but Now & Later's, Starburt, and mini sweet-tart packs (the kind that have 3 candy's inside) work great, as well as the toys used for pinatas. A few things like mini bags of MnMs don't work...too big.

2) Take a double layer of Duct tape and cover the speaker on the bottom!! The music is CRAZY loud and may be great for a loud party but UGH! the big-top music gets annoying very quickly. The double layer of tape makes it bearable.

Thet plastic bag wrapped around the crane claw was a little hard to remove (after reading another review on it being hard I recommend putting the batteries in the machine, move the crane all the way to the opening and move it all the way to the down position, then hold machine upside down and it was super easy to reach) ** HOWEVER, the 1st machine we ordered online that came to us broken, did NOT have the bag in place or the wire piece that locked it into posistion and I really do believe that was why it was cracked...bounced around and not protected during shipment.

Overall it is a great product, just wish it had a volume control. The music is used for it's timer. Music starts off normal, speeds up and then once the music stops, so does the movement of the crane with the ol' wah-wah-wah sound. And if you beat the music and drop candy in the slot, a little horn salute sounds. If there was a choice of lowering the volume I don't think I would have any complaints at all. Great fun!

What a hit on Christmas. My son loves claw games and when standing in line at a large toy store, I saw they sold claw machines. I went home and did my research on the different claw machines. I also found, if you want to end the sound quickly, all you have to do is tap the surface of where the prize goes out. Well, I actually didn’t find that out myself. Another similarity with all comments is broken controls. the only control that was broken was going front and back. I’m gonna have a family member check that out! Though it is fine without that certain control since the machine isn’t full. I think it just depends on where you put the prizes.

I bought toys and candy that were fairly light weight (plastic balls with toys inside, spikey mini balls, wrapped candy) and the claw picked all of them up just fine. I didn't expect a "real" carnival claw machine, I knew it would be plastic and maybe not perfect AND I wasn't disappointed with that expectation.  It came with the left to right action not working.  Usually this kind of thing is a matter of a short or a pinched wire - so I took all those little screws out and and tested each wire.  yep, one was pinched off - I repositioned, tested it again, hot glued it down and put it back together.  It works Ok for what we bought it for - a Carnival themed site at a music festival.  The music is annoying but hey, it's a game and stops when it runs out of time or you win, whichever first so it's livable.  Not bad for the $, but if I didn't know how to fix it, I would have been really ticked off - they need to quality check the items before packaging and shipping.  REALLY, how hard is that????

The kids get really in to it. I even found myself feeling like a kid again on the floor patiently waiting for a turn ;) We have not had any problems with it. The music is a bit loud, but I've read that there are YouTube tutorials to turn it off. I haven't tried because it really doesn't bother me. The kids can play with it on the floor and I can still easily hear the tv over the music. Although, quite honestly anytime they're playing with it I get so into it wondering if they're going to win a candy (sometimes two) that I don't have much interest in paying attention to anything else. I would definitely recommend this toy for kids ages anywhere from 5 to 95.

By far the best gift I have ever purchased for a birthday present. My seven-year old has been obsessed with crane games for the past two years and was beyond excited to receive this.

The first one I purchased had to be exchanged due to a faulty claw, but this current one has worked fantastically and has lasted through so many different children using it.

The only downside is the slightly annoying music.

Seriously - do a quick search for a home claw machine on Amazon and a dozen or so exact lookalikes will pop up.
There's literally NO real defining unique trait among them.

There IS however, a very common trait they all share. They are very cheaply made.

Honestly - I paid almost $40 for an item that was mostly defective - stopped working properly after ONE use.

I cannot in good conscious recommend this item to anyone - but to be fair, I cannot recommend ANY of the near exact same product from various different sellers you'll find on Amazon.

They clearly buy them at a low cost from Chinese manufacturers, and hope for the best.

Sorry to say, the quality is just not there.

I appreciate the idea behind this, but the quality speaks for itself.

Save yourself $30 or $40 and take your kid to the boardwalk and get ripped off there.
I'm sure they'll have more fun.

Where do I start?  It didn't work out of the box, and a chain piece was lying in the area where the candy goes.  I didn't want to return it without giving it a game try to fix it (kids were disappointed it had to go back), so I unscrewed the lid.  It appeared that during assembly in the factory, whoever put the lid on clipped one of the wires.  Easy enough to fix.  Still didn't work.  Unscrewed the base.  Found a wire disconnected that at one time perhaps was soldered to the on/off switch.  Pulled out the soldering gun, voila, the machine now works.  But hold on, the claw moves very poorly from left to right, and makes horrible noises.  Took apart the claw, only to find the metal bar was bent, causing the poor movemnet and noise.  Also, while the claw would raise, it wouldn't clamp shut when raised.  Aha, that's where that chain that was found goes, it was supposed to be attached to the claw.  That was enough for me, I boxed it up and back it went.  Game effort, but too many things wrong.  How could this get through inspection?

My 5 year old son loves, loves, loves this toy. He has not let it alone since he got it. It came with a bag of toys to fill it with, and a collection of plastic "coins" to activate each time. We've only had it a week, so I can't speak to its durability, but it works well so far. There are only a few downsides:

1. There is no off switch or volume control. The circus music becomes extremely annoying (to the parents) after an hour or so. I am going to duct tape over the speaker.

2. This only really works for small toys. Bigger or longer items (such as the woven finger trap) can't be dropped into the slot, so the child has to reach in and fish it out. Not a big deal, though.

3. I wish it just had a button to activate it, rather than the fake coins. The coins collect in a compartment in the bottom, so the child must periodically turn the machine over and open it up to empty it. There's no place to put the coins in the meantime. My son did discover that you can stick something pointy in the slot too, which works ok.

Overall, well worth the money (assuming it doesn't break tomorrow). My son shows everyone who comes in the house.

Prince dan here w another review and this time it's on The Electronic Claw Game. I have to say, I didn't expect this item to be so fun, and to receive it SO FAST, it's crazy. It arrived before the arrival date so that's a plus. The item came secure, however I did have some difficulty setting it up. First off, it's hard to remove the wire that's holding the claw in place. I had to remove it w tweezers and it took me a good ten minutes to do so, but eventually I got it. Then, there is a white wire that's sticks out when the claw machine moves around and when I would play it would get jammed against the slot you enter prizes in but nothing a little tape couldn't fix. I taped it against itself so that it wouldn't mess up. Have had it since sept 8, and it's still working till this day. I know it's only been a few weeks but maybe I'll post another review about it at a later time. For those wondering if you can use real money on it. Yes, you can. It's big enough to take in quarters so if you're looking to make some kind of profit off this, it's do-able. Does come w fake coins for those using it for fun. Haven't had major problems thus far, just minor that I've already explained. I'm sure if I have a problem w it, the owner wouldn't hesitate to exchange or refund, at least I hope. I'm loving this item and so are my family. Claw is very durable and holds items in place so it's very easy to win. However, make sure if you're the type to wanna make a profit off this that when the item falls through the slot that it lands in the slot area because if it falls out the slot opening, the player still gets to play again for a free second round. I don't know what is it but it just has to make that landing impact, but if you're just looking to buy this for fun then don't worry about this. This claw machine supports the use of 2-3 in toys so don't expect it to be huge. It's about a foot high. The handles have to be broken in. I thought I had broken it when I first used it, but don't worry. They have to make this pop sound because something inside is holding the handles in place I guess for shipping purposes so if you buy and it feels like the handle is stuck, just pull until it snaps inside so that you can fully utilize the handles. Hope this helps y'all.

I got this as a gag gift for a friend's ordination, then read all the cards to try to beat him at it. (We haven't played yet.) Of my motley group of not-very-religious friends, the ones who went to Catholic school and I thought it was oddly fun. My other roommates didn't understand the hilarity. Don't expect a 100% answer rate, even if you know your Bible.

I regret giving that copy away. The questions are fair trivia--neither utterly unknowable nor too obvious for the most part. About like playing Trivial Pursuit in one of the Genus Editions. Reading the citations for the answers (important feature!) sent me back to some Bible stories I either missed in Sunday School or didn't remember very well.

The set is a little dated in some ways--I gather that the interpretation of Gog and Magog as being Russia was rather specific to the time period. Also, left-wing Christians will find the emphasis a little odd in some places--if you like your Christianity to empower women, this is not the box set for you. If my implication that the Bible contains misogyny offends you, you will probably agree with the authors of the questions more than with me ;-).

Jazz is one of the best deluxe transformers in the movie line!I actually think Jazz looks alot cooler in the pontiac solstice mode compared to the G1 version.Jazz has a simple,but sleek design that is just awesome in either mode.There is great articulation in the legs,body,and head piece.Jazz is very realistic in either mode also to match how he is in the movie.Jazz comes with some sweet weapon features like a telescoping sword/gun for shooting down any decepticon attack and the spoiler section acts as a shield in robot mode for some defensive protection.The poseability is one of the best with great stability for acting out battle scenes or displaying this gem where you please.The durability is excellent as well with secure joints and a solid build in either robot or pontiac solstice mode to handle any situation.The transformation level is intermediate meaning if you have plenty of experience with transformers there is mid-level difficulty.If you are a parent buying for a child of 5-6 or younger you might have to help some in the process.Jazz honestly though has a very versatile and managable transformation all ages should be able to handle.The process starts off with the back part rotating down and underneath the front section.The same section then swings straight down with the lower parts where the wheels are sliding out.The same sections then rotate around with the feet folding out.The hood on the front splits into two sections while the front wheels rotate upwards to end up forming the arms.The top sections of the cab and spoiler then rotate down to rest on the back while the head piece is revealed in the same motion.The spoiler section can attach to the shoulder if you prefer a more battle ready look.If you are a fan of Jazz this is a must have for fans from the beginning or just starting out.Bottom line Jazz is a well-made,high quality,10 out of 10 transformer all fans will enjoy.I highly recommend Jazz to all transformer fans and collectors alike.Jazz Rocks and Transformers Rule!!!

I bought my first of these from our local grocery store.  Liked it so much that, when I was going to have pool guests, I ordered four more.  Inflation wasn't hard but, without a pump, did take a bit of time.  What I love about these is that they're absolutely great for 'desperate' heat moments -- and in Las Vegas, that's a good part of the summer.  Your feet and derrière hang in the water, you can 'sit', supported by the back of the float, or stretch out a bit more and rest your head easily... just in case you want to nap in the pool!  Nicest feature:  cup holders!  Drinks didn't spill.  I don't have to constantly mind the beverage -- it's not going anywhere.  If you want to float without any part of you getting wet, these aren't for you.  For Vegas, they're absolutely perfect.  And for the price, I can keep enough on hand for guests without investing a small fortune.

I have purchased about 20 of these over the past few years because they are cheap and I like the position it puts you in while in the water.  Keeps upper body out and lower body in.  Perfect for me.  BUT...they DO NOT LAST LONG.  Believe me when I tell you that the seat WILL separate from the rest of the float within about 2 months or so.  Sometimes faster.  My husband and I both use them. He is normal sized, and I am overweight.  I am VERY careful when I get in these because I know the tendency to break, right at the same point, every single time.  But alas,  no matter how careful you are, or how small [like I said husband is normal and one of them I specifically did NOT use to test and see if it was my weight, but that one broke too!] it will break. For price of less than eight which includes free shipping this is okay deal. NOT meant for kids to be hard on or try to swim through the seat opening as the 'seat' base is only attached to the topper U-shaped upper by heat bonding & our first of the 2 came lose after kids swimming through opening. Though I was able to repair...read on.

Both are on their second season & that's because I took some vinyl pool patch kit adhesive and cut some of the included clear plastic provided for doing patches (even under water) and created some reinforcement of the seams heat bonded seams.
I cut a long vinyl patch strip some 5-6 inches and after applying adhesive I glued 1/2 of ea. strip to one top edge of seat/upper connection area and folded over to sandwich in seam with other half glued to underside. I did this sandwich fold over reinforcement patch at all 4 seam points. I then took the adhesive and ran a bead of glue along the extra loose plastic all along the 10-12" seams to supplement the heat bonding all along where seat attached to the arms of the U-shaped upper. I repaired the torn seat in this same manner and it has held up for one week so far (Will update at end of season of use).

NOTE: though the photo does not necessarily show it...the upper arm areas do have large white handles plus at the round ends there are pocket openings for a can or drink built in to each arm. Also handy for suntan lotion sunglasses or for the daring: a water tight bag-sealed cell phone, etc.
If I get motivated I will try to include photo of my patching handiwork if it can be photographed with any accuracy.

Regardless, here is my review:

Use 5/5

I got one of these from Fry's marketplace and spent an entire Sunday in it. It has 2 cup holders. One is perfect for a smart phone (in a waterproof case) and the other for your drink. First thing I learned was that the bottom cushion should not be inflated too much (maybe 10%). That way my butt is in the water while it comfortably rests my back and head and it also helps prevent it from flying out of the pool and into my beautiful cacti if as I am too lazy to take it out of the pool when I'm done using it.

One of my favorite parts about this is how you can swim "frog-style" using just your legs to propel yourself around the pool if you decide to move around (counter-intuitive motion but works very well).

Durability 5/5

Only had it for a week and used it for about 5 hours so I don't know how long it's going to last but being this low cost, it doesn't really matter, I will get a few more for my wife and friends and hopefully that will keep it going.

Value 5/5

When my husband and I decided (about a decade ago) to put up a small inflatable ring pool in our backyard, I picked up a couple of these floats at a local store. They are perfect! You sit in the cool water but can lean back and rest your head. My hubby has actually fallen asleep while floating in it.

There are two reservoirs to fill with air, and it takes a little while...but an air compressor makes short work of it. The "seat" part tends to lose air quickly, but you can still use it just as well with that part deflated. They are cheap floats and not expected to last forever. As such we usually replace them just about once a year.

That being said, we keep going back to the same float because I haven't found anything else that works for us as well as this Sit-N-Float!

This tube is durable and comfortable. It was perfect for a sailing trip in the Greek Islands. After taking it out of the box, it fit nicely at the bottom of my duffel bag. The captain of our boat liked it so much that she's keeping it for next season! It even held up being tied to the side of the boat the entire trip. The only problem is we only brought one and everyone wanted to relax in it!

For the 5 minutes the Intex Sit N Float Lounge was used - it was great. It was perfect for floating in the pool with a beverage of choice in one of the holders and reading a Kindle or book. It kept your head out of water but the lower part wet to keep cool in 103 degree heat.

After floating into the side of the pool and pushing off gently with a foot, the right side of the seat tore away from the inflated side/back piece rendering the Lounge useless and the user very wet. The very small repair kit that is included with the item wouldn't begin to handle the repair job that was required so it was returned for a refund.

I think the fault is in the actual design of the float - the seat part is basically heat glued/attached to the smooth side of the back and arm piece and not sewn or heat seal-incorporated into any kind of seam that would make it stronger and more durable.

We're really disappointed that it failed because it had such promise.

Last year, after reading a zillion reviews on every floaty chair out there I got this one and one that was way more "high tech" (nylon webbing, everything was adjustable, fabric over plastic) and around $35. The $35 one? If I even THOUGHT about using the back rest part of it, I'd go over backwards into the water. No matter how I adjusted it, I couldn't use the backrest part or over I'd go. Gave it to some unsuspecting thin people. Maybe it'll be nicer to them.)

On this model one user complained about it not lasting for more than a couple of pool sessions. Another user said the trick was to not hyper inflate it and it'd last all summer, maybe next summer too. So I did the "not quite fully inflated" in the sling seat part as advised and almost fully inflated in the arms/back part (this allows the hot sun to expand the float without straining the seams). Man O Man! This floaty chair is the Bees Knees, the Cats Meow, the best thing since sliced bread, the best thing since Amazon Prime 2 Day Free Shipping (which is insanely good in itself).

First off, I'm a 5' 5" tall plus size lady, so if there's something tippy in the water I'm going to tip it over. It seems that most float rings, float triangles or float chairs are all made for children, or people who weigh under 150 pounds. Nice slender people. I got a triangle floaty thing (showed a lovely lady on the front of the package leaning back, in a comfortable seated upright position with lots of room on either side of her hips (she was probably 4' 11" and 95 lbs and on dry ground with water Photoshopped in). I tried THAT floaty thing. It looked as tho I fell butt first into a big flower pot with all apendages sticking straight up to the sky. It did NOT make a pretty picture. Thought I might have to cut my way out of it, but I didn't have my trusty knife with me (and no help to be seen).

There is no substitue for this awesome and most wonderful of floaty chairs. I bought 2 of these last year, but one blew away into the woods during a fall storm and the other one is still needing to be cleaned up a bit. So I got another one (cause they are that awesome, plus I like the new color). I call it the Queen Chair (it could also be called the King Chair. Bottom line is that it's MY chair if others are over :-D

So how come it's so great? Well, for starters it has those 2 white handles so that non-slender folks can keep it from scootching away while we try to get ourselves seated and situated. Then you can safely lean back and relax, with NO fear of tipping backwards. And it holds you in a comfortable, relaxed but upright position (K, for you skinny folks - this is WAY wider than a stupid airline seat where my thighs are pressed upwards and folded in). It has a nice wide seat which should fit almost anyone that's 325 lbs or less. It holds you in a fairly upright position (important if conversing with others or taking a sip of wine now and again). It doesn't let you sink too low in the water so you can easily reach the floaty table for your beverage, or you can use the handy cupholders for things like cans of pop or beer. I use the lid of a cheap styrofoam cooler as a floaty side table for beverages - they are very stable due to the shape, very cheap and last several seasons.

I don't know how this floaty chair works for slender folks but for plus size women (and guys too I'd say) it is the Bomb (ie Great).

Oh, and did I mention it is "slenderizng"? Yeppers, you look lots thinner while floating in this chair. All that and it's under $10 bucks. Can't be beat, in my opinion.

This is billed as a toy, but it's more of an adult's float chair. Holds a 250 lb man with no problem in a seated position in the water, with a place to hold a cold drink.

Reasonably well made, very good value for the economical price. The seller shipped very fast.

Based on some reviews I was concerned that this float wouldn't last the day, but it survived all day out on the water with lots of use. In fact, it was such a hit I had to wait over an hour just to get "my turn" to try out my own float! I was looking for a float that was more like a chair and less like a hammock, and this fit the bill perfectly. (The only other thing that came close was the "water saddle", which if you can get past it looking like a giant diaper, still requires effort to stay upright and seated.) This float was exactly what I was looking for - something that puts you in a comfortable upright position but also allows you to lounge a bit. When the tide started going out we used the handles on the arms to tie to the boat - worked great. Originally I wasn't even looking for something with cup holders, now I can't imagine going without them!

Durability -
I've only used it once so far, but it endured a lot of travel and not so delicate handling. It went from inflated in the back of the car, then into the trunk of another car, then onto the boat where it was stowed (shoved) into the cabin, and finally into the water where it got lots of use - and then all the way back again. I did follow another reviewer's suggestion to not overfill it, and maybe this helped. I didn't have any problems with the seat.

It was a little larger than I expected. Definitely adult sized. After I first inflated it I didn't think I would fit it into the car, but it managed to fit everywhere just fine. At home I rinsed it off with the hose, deflated it, hung it out on the clothesline to dry and then folded it up (easily), and put it away to use next time. For the price I really only cared that it lasted the day, so it exceeded my expectations.

I purchased these with the plans of prototyping a couple game ideas and working with my kids on counting.

The colors shown did not exactly match the picture. (I got a light green instead of a light blue which is no big deal). But it was 10 different colors @ 50 each as stated.

Very happy with the purchase.

The cubes are exactly what they say they are. One centimeter a side, hard plastic, multiple colored cubes. They are not heavy, but I'm not sure if they are hollow or solid.

These make great markers for boardgames or miniatures games. I've used them to mark destroyed bridges and victory conditions in wargames. As I said, they are light, and they can slide across the game board if they catch your sleeve or if the board is hit hard enough to make the counters or "bits" move.

I've also experimented with converting some of them to dice to use as a substitute for the (overpriced) custom dice that come with many games. The cubes are really too small to hold an elaborate pattern that can be read from any distance, and they don't have the heavier feel of standard dice. On the other hand, you can take a fine Sharpie to them and make a basic number, letter, or design, and they will function like normal dice. And you get a ton of them for about the price of one set of custom dice that are sold for some games. If you want to use these cubes as dice, understand the trade off.

I had seen this same cast puzzle at a friend's house and it was so solid and awesome that I came on amazon to get one for myself. Apparently though the Hanayama cast is no where near the quality of the real Japanese puzzles. The one I got feels cheap and light in my hand, and it doesn't close properly. The little mechanism that keeps it together doesn't fit how it should because it is so flimsily made. I would recommend skipping out on this one, because as fun as it is, it is so poorly made it ruins the puzzle. The stainless steel puzzles I have gotten from hanayama hold up much better.
Awesome interactive puzzle, but with intuition it took me about 2 mins to solve. Thank you! I'll have fun see others struggle.

This puzzle is what got me hooked on Hanayama puzzles.  A friend pulled it out of his pocket and started manipulating it in his hands.  I was fascinated by the look of it, the intrigue it offered as well as its feel in my hands.  I actually guessed at it's correct solution almost immediately, yet it failed to yield to my confident attempts to defeat it.  I had failed to anticipate the rest of the solution.  With this failure behind me, I went on to spend several days looking in the wrong directions for its solution, turning it around in my hands, listening to its mechanisms, trying to learn its secrets.  I found the experience fascinating, knowing it held a solution that was not yet available to me.  That period of frustration and anxiety is precious, knowing that it can never be recreated once the solution is known.  I have since purchased a number of other puzzles and am awaiting several that I just ordered.  But for me, they will all pale before NEWS which, while perhaps not the most rewarding puzzle, or the most manipulable, still the feel, the appearance, the mystery and the entertainment it produces in those who encounter it is unmatched.

I have about a half dozen different Hanayama cast puzzles. My favorites are the ones with no hidden mechanism, like the Labyrinth rings: you can manipulate it and see everything about the puzzle, but still can't quite figure it out. This one (Cast News) falls into the category of hidden trick. Once you get it apart you see the "trick" and it's pretty easy to reproduce at will and is not as much fun to play with later on.

Like all Hanayamas this one has nice solid construction.

Hanayama cast metal puzzles are fantastic and devious brain teasers. News, however, is something of a departure for the series, and isn't one of my favorites. The puzzle's mechanism is hidden inside, and you cannot see the results of your manipulations while attempting to solve it.

This is very unusual for the series, which usually let you see every piece of the puzzle while you work at it. That being said, it is still a quality puzzle.

I couldn't be happier with both choices. The cards themselves are top quality. We have used them for a year, and there are no imperfections whatsoever. The all-plastic material feels nice when shuffling. Once you've used these cards, you will never be able to go back to plastic-coated paper cards. I recommend the jumbo index, as they are much easier for my 46-year old eyes to read. The older folks in the game feel the same way.

A couple of words about the seller, Stay at Home Dads. I asked for specific color cut cards, and the seller was very accommodating. The cards were shipped quickly and are priced competitively, especially with the cut cards thrown into the bargain. I will happily buy from this seller again.

UPDATE 08/23/14: These cards are still playable, but after almost five years of use every Sunday in our poker game, I've bought another set of these KEM cards. They are the best cards I've ever owned and have survived both humid summer nights (no air conditioning at the host's home) and sticky fingers (one of the player's is quite messy with the snacks). The seller provided excellent customer service.

My husband and I are Lego fans, and I have been building Lego sets since I was a kid myself, so when we saw this set, we decided to get it to build it together with our 5-year-old. We got this set because she loves constructing houses out of different materials and we thought she might enjoy trying this out - which turned out to be true. I consider this set as value for money because there are three different house designs that can be constructed, and each design comes complete with detailed instructions.

Since she is still quite young, we don't get very far each time we work on this model, but that's ok - it's a learning process, and it teaches her patience too. Usually, we will stop when she gets tired, and give her 'free' Legos (which we have in abundance) so that she can give free rein to her imagination. Our friend's child who is about 8 also loves Legos, and he has one of these sets. He was able to put it together without much help. I'd recommend this set as a perfect, educational and creative toy for both boys and girls.

My brother-in-law and I just recently joined a D&D group that a friend of ours is starting, and thus, we needed to purchase some dice. My brother-in-law got a set of Chessex dice from a local gaming shop, and they were quite nice. But since I was ordering the books I needed from Amazon anyways, I figured I might as well purchase mine online. It was cheaper that way, and it was the same brand, so I figured there would be no issues.

I was wrong. The d20 die included in this set lacks the underscoring needed to tell the difference between the 6 and the 9. Ironically enough, every other die in the set has the underscoring: even the d6 and d8, which don't need it because they don't have a 9.

In addition, several of the dice have noticable nicks or scratches, possibly damage that occured during shipping. These aren't too bothersome, but they are a slight annoyance, especially when added to the issue of the d20. Though it's funny, because the damage actually makes the d20 usable because it's possible to tell the 6 and 9 apart by the distinct nicks each face has.

Overall, the set was a definite disappointment.

No, my dice don't have speckles. No, my dice don't have glitter or contain mini galaxies or have swirls or unicorn farts or glow in the dark.
My dice are carved from the bones of my fallen enemies and they don't give a $#&@ about your tragic backstory.
My dice are here to clear dungeons, disable traps, slay monsters and sway opinions. They don't need or even want to be pretty.
They are bringers of epic triumph and crushing defeat. They can not be reasoned with. They can not be swayed. It is possible they may be fudged from time to time.
They are not here to be glamorous.
They are here to determine destinies.
They don't have time for your s#+@.
They come in a set of ...SEVEN!
THEY COME IN A CLEAR CONTAINER!
THEY ARE... CLASSIC IVORY CHESSEX DICE!!!
That is all.

I'm pissed. I ordered with Prime delivery. I remember when that actually meant two day delivery. I ordered this 4-5 days before Christmas and now I learn that it won't come until the Tuesday after. (Christmas was on a Sunday). So this, to be fair, was ordered during the Christmas rush, but even so how is a week's delivery any better than standard UPS? I am mad at myself because I ordered from a different vendor and received it in 2 days. These vendors offer the same product at the same price, and I thought that I WAS in fact ordering from the same vendor because they had gone to some trouble to deliver promptly. (I ordered one about 2 days before the second order) The product is fine, and the case is way more solid than I expected. Honestly, this is totally as heavy as anyone would care to transport farther than across the family room. The cards look like "BEE", (a very good American poker/casino sized, cloth playing card set now owned by Bicycle but with better face and ace designs than the Bicycle version) but they are not. You get "BCG" instead, which I suspect is supposed to stand for "Bicycle Club Special". This looks like Bee to the casual glance. The box, typeface and even the card back design looks like Bee until you turn them over. These are paper cards, not cloth and do not have the Bee faces. The dice are a mid-range in quality, better than expected. My choice, but I would like a small set like this to have colour proportions to be 3-2-1, white-red-blue. Whatever game we play there are never enough white ones.

Cliffjumper finally has a kick-ass camaro mode with one decepticon smokin robot mode!This is the best Cliffjumper deluxe version done to where it still has the solid red color for the most part like Cliffjumper having secure joints while being able to handle whatever comes around.I actually own all the Bumblebee versions that are the same mold and have had no problems at all and Cliffjumper has been the same, A solid all around transformer that is one of the best.The transformation level is advanced meaning if you have plenty of experience with transformers there is mid-level difficulty.If you are a parent buying for a child of 5-6 or younger you might have to help out some.Honestly Cliffjumper is a very versatile transformer for all ages to be able to learn with some practice.The process starts with the doors swinging out while the roof seperates.The front tire sections then pull out.The back section seperates next while the windshield comes down.The front section of the camaro then folds over to form the chest and reveal Cliffjumper's head.The front tire sections then fold inwards onto the back while the arms then form at the side.The feet are formed by rotating the back sections of the camaro around so the sections are to the back of the leg while the forward panel rotates up.The last step is to just staighten the leg out in a downward motion.Cliffjumper is one of the most daring and brave autobots ever and this version does him proud.Bottom line Cliffjumper is a well-made,high quality,10 out of 10 transformer all fans will enjoy.I highly recommend Allspark Power Cliffjumper to all transformer fans and collectors alike.Cliffjumper Rocks and Transformers Rule!!!

First I tried to take the sticker off the container, it only tore off the top layer and left basically an entire sticker still on there, which when I tried to scrape off became hopeless to remove, so it looked terrible. Then when trying to remove the top of the container, the sticker prevents a clean removal of the lid, and the rim of the clear plastic part that fits into the black lid broke off inside the black lid part. So the container became useless to hold the dice 5 mins after receiving the product (someone else in the reviews complained of the same thing I just noticed). Then I figured ok I'll just use a different container to hold these or even a ziploc bag not the end of the world. Then I poured the dice out into a tray, checked a few of them rolling around, all seemed ok. Until I went to put them back in the container. I realized that two dice I assumed were rolled close together, were in fact STUCK together. Not like, by some glue or something, but as if they had been carved from the same piece of material and never separated. I tried to snap them apart, which I could not do surprisingly because of their small size. I figured it wouldn't have mattered as even if I snapped them apart they would not have rolled correctly so I was already out 2 dice from my cube. Very disappointing, will return.

These dice are nicely packaged and were the perfect size for the dice cups that we use to play liar's dice. Normal size dice were a bit too large to roll in the cups, so we decided to buy a set of 12mm dice.

All 36 dice arrived in the cube in perfect condition. The red pips on all of them (as far as I can tell without physically turning each one over and over) are placed correctly. I have uploaded a picture to give people who may not be familiar with 12mm dice an idea of their size.

Overall, these dice are very nice and, for the price, they are the best you can get.

I like the dice, it's Chessex quality. They however are the wrong dice. Product described is VORTEX. I recieved GEMINI.
Both sets of dice are purple with gold pips so I am willing to overlook the mistake.
It doesn't change the fact that it IS NOT what I ordered. I SPECIFICALLY compared VORTEX and GEMINI when I was picking out the dice. They ARE different and IT DOES MATTER. Please be more careful in the future.

I bought a bunch of these swords for a pirate party I gave for my son. I didn't give it 5 stars for the following reasons:

1. The chemical smell was horrible when opening these. Luckily I opened one a week before the party and was alerted to the issue so had time to open them all and let them air out (outside). By the day of the party they were fine.
2. Not sure what you call it but where you blow it up wasn't consistent. On some the piece had some plastic on the other side so after you filled it up with air (squeezing the piece while you blew) you could close it without losing too much air. On some the plastic piece wasn't there so it was tricky trying to keep your finger covering the opening and closing it fast enough to not lose too much air.

I still gave the swords 4 stars because they were the best value out there and I was able to order the qty I wanted instead of for example a pack of 12. They were safe and fun to play with and a big hit with the kids. I would buy them again for a fun party give away.

I don't get why this has so many great reviews.  My 5 year old loves words and spelling and so Hangman is a natural favorite of hers… but the tiles are way too hard to turn over! Might as well just use pen and paper.  There's absolutely no difference in the game except this is just fancy.  My daughter is sitting next to me still trying to turn the tiles over after playing.
Good toy for playing in the car or in some other space where you don't want to lose any pieces

I have an easier time than he does, but for me it is quite difficult too.  Maybe when we break the game in and play more often they will loosen up,

This is a great way to play hangman. No wasting of paper and it's so solid it can be used anywhere. My six year old grandson likes to play this in church. The only drawback is the snapping sound of the "tiles" as they are turned over. I took care of that by putting self-adhesive felt tabs on the backs of the "tiles". That doesn't work on the printed side, but I'm going to try some clear rubber "dots" for those sides. Of course an adult can turn the tiles over without making much noise, but the younger ones have a harder time if they aren't very patient. If noise isn't a factor, then you'll like this a lot.

This is about the size of an average size of paper though thicker and heavier of course.  It works great for traveling or waiting around a doctor's office.  I don't think the marker erases as thoroughly as I would like but even with a slight smudge appearance it still works fine.

We all love Melissa & Doug toys..the only reason I didnt give this one 5 stars is that the bands holding the pieces are really tight (they do begin to stretch a bit as the game is used) and it caused a few "owies" for my grandson as we started to use the game...the bands seem to stretch a bit over time

Actually, I did not get this toy from Amazon, but as a gift.  However, we are prime members and I wanted to write a review because this has been one of the best toys for my 6 year old gifted son, and it's really not that expensive.  He wants to take this everywhere and play it with everyone, which is why I recommend it.  Yes, you can play hangman on any old piece of paper, but this set is highly portable, fun, and easy to use for little hands who are not that adept at handwriting or drawing yet - just flip over the letters and parts of the 'hang-man' which are attached with elastic cord - no choking if you have little ones like we do.  I do not think he would have liked the game so much without this version.  We love it.

Excellent game!  I practice my first grader's weekly word study words with this game almost daily.  I love that all pieces are attached, even a wipe off pen is included with a secure rubber band attachment.  I believe this game will be played with for years to come.

I bought two of these Hangman games for my nieces this year & it has been a huge hit. (One in 1st grade & one in 2nd) It's well made because you can't lose any of the pieces & great for in the car. Educational, "old school" fun!

This is a great way to play hangman, especially while in the car! The three year old doesn't play this yet but likes to steal the marker from his sisters & doodle on the whiteboard portion of the game.  The 8 & 9 year old do great with making up words for each other and playing the game.  I like the fact that the "man" is already designed for you so there is no guessing how many other "parts" can be added before he is hung because he is "all there" already.  I also like that the marker has it's own storage spot on the side of the board and that when the marker dries out, it can be easily replaced with other whiteboard markers or whiteboard crayons.

This is the BEST game. It is a necessity for travelling in the car, or anywhere there is a wait with the kids. All letters are attached, and FLIP when named. The bottom is a dry erase board. NO MISSING PIECES OR CLEAN-UP. Helps with vocabulary & spelling.

I don't get it- you have to carry around the board and the pen...what is the difference between this and simply bringing a pen and pad of paper?  I was disappointed. However, we frequently play the "old-fashioned" hangman in waiting rooms, restaurants, etc. ...when it just isn't convenient to carry around the board.  Overall, a waste of money!

Originally I bought this for my dance recital for our butterfly dance but they aren't predictable. They do what they say they do, but you never know where they will fly off too :) The kids love them!

The butterfly was much bigger than I imagined it would be. About half the size of a paperback book, maybe bigger.

It does not fly anywhere near 20 feet into the air, but it does launch nicely and makes a buzzy fluttering sound when it does.

It will definitely cause your "victim" to jump and possibly drop anything they are holding (including the card), so keep that in mind.

Everything is plastic, including the wings. And of course there is a rubber band.

It can be re-used over and over.

The butterfly received was not the same color as the one in the picture and you have to wind it up a bit more than what it says in the instructions but other than that it worked great and fluttered right out of the card when it was opened.  I am tempted to get a few more to use in the future.

Bubbles bubbles everywhere. I actually took the time to photograph my wretched bubble filled d20 and contact Chessex for a replacement. Ironically, the replacement d20 arrived and it too was so full of bubbles as to be illegible. Now folks, it's time to admit to ourselves that things have changed. Gone are the days of buying immaculate dice at high prices from Chessex. The quality is gone in favor of high priced trash.

Now it only makes sense to purchase wizdice by the pound from China. You'll get around fifteen complete sets for around a dollar each. The quality is better the price is a fraction. Chessex has no product anymore.
Very disappointed with these dice. They are not nearly as easy to see as the dice in the picture. It's very hard to view the numbers on the dice unless you play outside, in direct sunlight.

Considering the low price of these dice. I wasn't really sure these could be my main set of dice. However, they looked good in pictures and I'm a sucker for great looking dice.

I've been pleasantly surprised at how much I really like them.

I examined each of them closely when they arrived, looking for any sign of imperfection. They look and feel great. No imprint misalignment of numbers, edges were clean, and the numbers are easy to see in normal light. If I have one complaint about them, it would be that it is fairly hard to tell they are translucent without holding it up to the light. However, I feel it's because of the smoky color more then anything else. I think it would be unfair to knock off a * for it.

With all that being said, the most important aspect would obviously be how well they roll. They have all been excellent in the randomness of the numbers. In gaming with them and killing time, I've yet to see any sort of pattern appear.

Overall, I'm very happy with this purchase and I will be buying the other similar dice from Chessex, based off of this set.

First of all: I adore Reaper paints, Reaper miniatures...heck, I even applied for a job in Denton, TX once upon a time knowing that I'd be closer to the Reaper warehouse & store if I got it (I didn't, c'est la vie). I'm only disclosing this so that you know I am a firmly biased reviewer with nary an ill word for my favorite mini company.

That being said, Reaper's weak spot, paint-wise, has in my experience been their metallics. I would paint my miniatures almost exclusively with Reaper paints *except* for the metallic bits, which I would coat with GW product. I gave these a try because the price was right, and I am (like so many other hobbyists) sick of paying so much for GW metallics only to have them dry out before I even get halfway through the pot.

Well, Reaper exceeded my expectations. The flow is fine (that was never a problem), and the coverage is radically improved form what I've experienced. Rather than looking like yellow paint with flecks of glitter (like hobby paint from Michael's / Hobby Lobby), the dried product looks much more convincingly like metal at 25mm scale.

Again, if you tried Reaper metallics in the past and found the coverage poor or the final coat glittery, give them another go - I'm very happy I did!
I already had a gold paint. The gold triad allowed me to quickly create the aged bronze look.

Seems like an OK standard servo; I've had one as the rudder/nose wheel servo in my Eflite Apprentice ever since I got it as a RTF trainer. I've had no trouble with it, though it seems noisy. My biggest issue is the mounting holes that are molded into the servo's construction; they're simply round holes rather than the "C" shaped opening that you find on almost every other servo out there. It doesn't come with nor can you mount the standard rubber grommets with brass inserts that also come with ever other servo out there. It mounts in the foam Apprentice with silicone caulk. Basically, it's just glued down. Also, I can't seem to find specs for it anywhere. Nothing about torque or rotational speed. It does not seem overly fast, so I guess its moniker of "standard servo" fits pretty well. I say "get something else" because for the money that Eflite is asking for the 7150, you could get something else better from a name-brand servo manufacturer.

I will say, however, that when I got my Eflite T-34 trainer, it came with 7120 servos, all of which buzzed and stuttered something awful. I called Horizon Hobby about it and they promptly sent me four brand new 7150 servos as replacements. But see the mounting issue as to being able to use them in the T-34 kit. Horizon Hobby's service is awesome, but this servo is pointless.

I'll tell you what! I paid $14 for this gun at a Bob Evans while visiting Florida, and felt it was worth it! At half the price here on Amazon, this is probably one of the best quality toys you can find for the money. And even today, I don't regret paying $14.

As one reviewer stated, it's apparent these guns come in different colors. He received a red one (thinking he was getting the pictured blue), and the one I purchased was black. This Amazon seller ships whatever color is in stock, but please don't let this deter you from ordering. Whatever color you get is worth it.

Most toy guns in this price range are always that cheap, hard plastic. Not that this one isn't plastic, it is. However, the grip has almost like a rubbery texture, and the entire gun does not have that Dollar Store look and feel. In my adult hand, the gun is full size. In the smaller hand of a 6-year old, it's an awesome size ready to take on any creatures from this earth or beyond.

The pictures do not do the light effects justice. They are really cool. Personally, I was mesmerized by all the different effects right to the back of the gun. The sound effects are crisp, clear, but quite loud. It does get obnoxious when one of the kids just sits there holding the trigger for like 30 straight seconds.

This is one of the more popular toys in my son's "Guns and Weapons" collection. The kids anxiously wait their turn to play with it. Worth the money, and more.

When I first saw this 1,000-piece puzzle on Amazon I liked it so much that I ordered it, but neglected to notice that it measured 24 inch x 30 inch. So despite being 1,000 pieces, it did not fit on my terrific Jigboard! However, my ingenious daughter enlarged the Jigboard by adding an extension. This sort of worked. Once I realized the puzzle was so large, I did see, on one side of the box, that the pieces are extra large! I didn't see this noted anywhere in the puzzle's description, or would probably have been more careful and checked measurements before buying. Missing this, however, was my fault. The puzzle is fascinating, I loved seeing all the old kitchen items. However, I gave it only three stars because a lot of the pieces required "deburring" (removal of pieces of cardboard on bottom of piece) before they would fit. This should be done by the manufacturer, not the person working the puzzle. In addition, there are many pieces that fit together only with a curve, there is no actual interlocking of the pieces, and until the surrounding pieces are in place, these move all over the board! My final complaint is regarding the colors of the pieces making up the puzzle. They are not a good match for the picture, e.g., a lavender puzzle piece may appear off-white on the cover illustration. I love puzzles, and hoped to buy several more from White Mountain, but will not be doing so now.

Well Since Tamiya announced they were releasing this kit, we modelers have been champing at the bit.
I got this kit in my grubby paint and glue stained paws, only to realize I have a problem...

I want more!!!

This kit is the Grail we Viper lovers have been looking for.
Truthfully it is not just a Block 30 - 32 - 42 and N
But a Block 25, 30, 32, 40, 42, 50, 52 all rolled into one box.
Granted you will have to figure out the parts for where they go.. But that is the fun...
The Pilot LOOKS GOOD!!! that is not a first for a kit
But a plus when you think you want to detail the cockpit with someone in it.
With the panels that you have to fill this means that other variants are in the works.
Crisp and clean lines, sharp detailing in the engraving as well as a wonderfully rendered gear bay...

Be cautious with this one as the biggest problem is, the seam on the top of the canopy as well as the radome being in two parts...
Careful sanding and a dip in Future will take care of the seam line.
The radome needs to be joined with sparring amounts of adhesive.

Detail Up Parts are available for this kit, they include the various reinforcement plates (Lawn Mower Blades, or scab plates) as well as metal AOA Probes and Pitot Probe.

Some Observations...
1 Bit pricey -- but worth it
2 No Weapons outside the Aim-9's and Aim-120's -- Hey it is an Aggressor / Adversary it don't need them...
3 Static Discharge strips on the Radome are all but eliminated when you sand it -- Be Careful, A Little Tamiya Tape and some Putty will give them back...
4 Static Dischargers missing from the airframe -- well in 1/48th scale they would be about as thin as the fiber from a toothbrush... Hint Hint Hint.
5 There is No Number 5...
6 When all else fails... Research your subject... several websites are available to help you find pictures, reference for paint as well as any quirks in the airframes.

I hope this helps you with your Future Viper purchase

I bought this because I love the Human Alliance Bumblebee figure. As it turns out, the Human Alliance Skids figure is even better. The car mode is okay. It would be great, except that the doors don't have a way to lock into place when they are closed in car mode. Therefore, the doors may or may not align properly with the rest of the car, and can feel a little bit loose. I would rate the car mode a "9".

Where the figure really shines though, is in the robot mode. It is incredibly detailed, and extremely poseable. The transformation is really complicated, but also a lot of fun. After I transformed him the first time, it took a while for me to pose him in a way that I liked, but once I figured it out, it looks extremely good. I think overall, it actually looks better than the Human Alliance Bumblebee figure. The one thing I didn't like about the Human Alliance Bumblebee figure is that if you turn him around and look at him from the side, there is a huge gap in the middle. This isn't the case with Skids. One thing that people don't like about this figure is that it has door wings, while the CGI didn't. Initially it bothered me, but now that I'm used to it, it doesn't bother me at all. I rate the robot mode a "10".

The Arcee motorcycle figure looks like crap in robot mode, so I just keep it in the motorcycle mode, which is actually decent. The Mikaela figure is okay. I think it's about the best that can be done, giving how small it is. It is extremely poseable though, and it does complement the Sam figure nicely. In any case, you're probably just getting the set for the Skids figure anyway, and the Skids figure, as I said, is excellent!

A friend gave me a slightly used Zoom H4n portable digital recorder so I could record my music. I decided to purchase the remote control for increased convenience. It was shipped from Japan, so understandably it took a while to arrive. I was pleasantly surprised when I opened the box to find it also included note from the seller, a small gift made by the seller's child, and a piece of candy. This warm, human, and personal touch is very rare in the modern world I know, and was greatly appreciated. Not only was the price of the remote control very reasonable, but it has been useful in my recordings by reducing handling noise and allowing more convenient control of the recorder. It has been money well spent.

Nice to be able to control my H4n from a distance.  I use it as a backup audio track for videotaping events and recording our praise band.  Nice that it also came with an extra extension cable.  The only downside I can see is lack of strain relief where the cable enters the remote.  Don't know how it will hold up in the long run.

The cord on this is long, and that comes in handy, when you want to operate from extreme locations. Obviously, if you need more length, an added cable could help, but this reaches across my living room, so it will do some good field work.

This remote may seem like a luxury but it isn't. When doing interviews the last thing you want to hear is fumbling noises as you start to record, or pause. If there is a kit for the H4n including the remote buy it! Well worth the money, too bad it's not included with the recorder though.

This is a well made, but unnecessarily large remote control for the Zoom recorder. I use it with my H4N, and have had problems. The cord is a decent length. I will​ replace it if I can find a wireless option. Until then, it will continue to be a solid addition to my sound kit.
gooooood (:

Absolutely brilliant!  The Zoom H4n is a brilliant machine to record (for me) playing guitar and singing, to hear how it sounds and make improvements.  The Remote is essential,  really so you don't have to get up and down to start and stop the recording, and the microphone is very sensitive, so you tend to record the manual switching on and off.

It is hard to complain about this remote since it does exactly what I expected when I bought it but using it a bit makes you wish the could have included a few extra functions, esp. since this is pretty pricy for just a little remote. I do think it's a good product but I also find a few annoyances pop up for me from time to time.

For starters, I really wish the thing had a low battery indicator. I can estimate the Zoom's recording time but it sure would be nice to have some visual warning as well. Generally, if you are using a remote, the recorder is where you can't see it.

Secondly--and this is a bit more involved--but a somewhat better level meeter (perhaps three or five LEDs instead of just one) would be really helpful. You can adjust levels on the thing but you can really only tell if you are red-lining or almost red-lining and while that is workable, it leaves a little more guesswork than I would like. Again, if you are using the remote, you generally can't see the recorder well enough to use the meters.

Thirdly, I wish this had a control lock separate from the one on the recorder itself. If you lock the controls on the recorder, the remote doesn't work either. Typically, I just make sure the buttons on the recorder aren't in any danger of being pressed but in the rare cases where that's not possible, it renders this thing essentially useless. I do also have problems where I accidentally hit buttons but that happens rarely enough that it's practically a non-issue.

This all being said, this little remote does allow me to do what I bought it for, which is to allow me to start and stop recording while I have the Zoom recorder packed away in my bag. I have found it to be quite durable and even to be reasonably water resistant. (I would not dunk it in water but I don't worry about getting rain on it.) Overall, it is a bit expensive but it has served me very well, which I hope is apparent by my four star rating. I just wanted to point out a few problems in case they are deal breakers for anyone.

I shoot videos in small club settings and try to mount my Zoom H4N as high as I can to capture as little audience noise as possible. I purchased this wired remote toward that goal. All of the features on this remote work just fine, except there are no level meters on it to allow me to monitor gain settings on the four channels I'm recording. Since the Zoom is mounted above my head, it's not as though I can use the meters on the recorder (which in 4 channel mode are unusably small anyway. Yes, I know the little green LED lights turn from green to orange to red to indicate the approach of then the onset of clipping, but that simply isn't enough. I need to know levels during quieter passages too - and the orange and red lights don't seem to render properly when fed a line signal (which, btw the Zoom doesn't handle right anyway since its expected "line" level input threshold is really an instrument level - but I digress).

I'd gladly have paid another $100 for usable level meters in this remote, or even for an accessory level indicator meter unit which I could run inline of this remote - but Zoom doesn't offer one. So I muddle through with what I have and always run a "just in case" audio feed to the camera from my field mixer.

The product matches the picture. The hilts are gray plastic and lack the "chrome" sheen on the lightsabers you get with the Lego sets.

The blades themselves are also a different color. The green is much yellower and the blue is a little lighter and greener than what you get in Lego sets. The red seems to be the same.

Still, they are fully compatible with any Legos you may have. If you have a younger child, I suggest gluing the blades into the hilts (I use hot glue, but super glue works) so that they don't get lost. A tiny dab of either glue works fine -- you want to be able to fit the blade into the hilt.

I sure do! First of all, as the mother of a LEGO the Clone Wars/Star Wars fanatic I can only say, what a GREAT idea to package up the extra light sabers to sell!

Soo....by Amazon category:

1. How much fun is this toy? Ummm, light sabers are always fun, particularly the LEGO ones as they go with the minfigs :P. Fun by definition almost!
2. Educational Value? Very Educational. Teaches parents to keep small parts in the back-up reserve pile.
3. Durable? Very. They must be as they seem to grow legs and walk away. Must be very tough to go hide with all the missing socks and gloves.
4. Overall--All Five Stars.

Okay, I'm being silly, but really, having the extra light sabers is a great idea for when young, creative minds decide to invent new things as well as to replace small items that go missing.

I also must admit that I did read the 1 star ratings and was a little startled at how many there were. I wouldn't ding a product based on a bad seller--that's what seller's feedback is for. It doesn't help describe an item at all. In any case, the vendor may vary from the one I used (it was an Amazon partner, not Amazon), so check the sellers feedback before you order. Also, I was surprised that anyone would have expected a larger toy. Perhaps they did not realize they were ordering LEGO pieces.

So to sum up...

HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!

The models themselves are fine but the issue is that this set only comes with 20 boltspitters for 24 models, and it doesn't tell you this anywhere on the box or the manual. This is particularly annoying because Skinks get plus 1 to hit in a unit of 20 or more, so if you split them into two units of 12, you lose this bonus, and if you do one unit of 20 boltspitters, you have 4 skink models that are basically useless. The javelins, shields, and clubs all have enough parts for 24 models, it's just the boltspitters that are short 4.

This is unfortunately the result of Games Workshop being lazy and re-using old sprues from WFB for AoS (these skink sprues were first printed in 2002). I guess nobody bothered to look over the sprues to make sure there were enough of each weapon type.

Before I review the actual toy, I want to say that this is the very first "real" (in my 5 year old's words) Barbie that we have purchased. These Barbies aren't even close to the level of quality, detail, and durability of the dolls I had when *I* was a girl. So my 5-star rating is based on the clearance price I paid at the store (it's unfair that these dolls retail for as much as they go for; and dolls with legs that can actually bend SHOULDN'T cost more!), and that this Barbie has great clothes (easy to change, very ladylike outfit, AND she's actually wearing low-heeled sneakers!!) and super cute accessories & pets.

Again, the Potty Training Pups Barbie I purchased was on *clearance*. So aside from paying less than half-price, I also only received 2 of the 3 dogs shown here (the one left out was the itty bitty one with the two bows that "functions" when squeezed). This wasn't at all an oversight from the seller - the box I have clearly displays only two dogs in the imagery and the little story in the back. Also, the Barbie included in the set was missing her necklace that is shown here. Again, the picture of Barbie displayed on the box shows her without it as well. So, obviously Mattel shipped Barbie in this "alternate" packaging; whether because of cost cutting, or they didn't manufacture enough puppies, or whatever. Not a big deal, but if I had paid full price and didn't get exactly what I had ordered as it had been described, I would definitely not be entirely happy. But other than that, all the other accessories (doggy bowl, toys, plastic sheets) were all accounted for.

As has already been mentioned in another review, the puppy toys are TINY... smaller in fact that those little throw-away plastic rings and Silly Bands that kids wear. Definitely either supervise your toddler child (anyone under 4) when they play with this toy, or have an "inventory check" of the little items after playtime especially if you have pets and little kids around the house.

So after years of bucking the 50-plus-years-and-going-strong mother-daughter pasttime, I finally caved and bought my daughter her first Barbie. Considering all of the headache-inducing choices available now, I will definitely be more choosy as to exactly which dolls, clothes, and accessories we spend our toy money on. But as a "starter Barbie," this was definitely a great purchase!

I've been suckered into buying puzzles with great pictures that turned into a head-banging experience. Not this one. When I work on my Helis, I always use Locktite on the screws that are metal to metal. This helps keep them from vibrating loose and ending up falling out. The unfortunate reality is that one will still end up missing. I've rarely had them fall off in flight, but it happens. Where I have lost them has been when I try to do an in-field repair for some minor issue, the screw ends up missing in the grass or dirt in the park.

A big tip, use a towel or cloth of some sort when working on your heli on a park bench. That really helps to keep from loosing one.

This set has been great to keep on hand. I've also use some when I've stripped the socket out due to it being in just a little too tight. Having this keeps me in the air and works out nicely.

This reproduction is very well made. I don't know if I would be able to tell the difference between it and a true vintage doll. When considering buying a reproduction Barbie doll, look at her hands. Both thumbs and pinkies should be separate from the other fingers, and the forefinger on her right hand should also be separate. There should be nail polish on each of the fingers and toes. This doll's hands are perfect--the fingers even have life-like contours on the under sides of the fingers. And, as with the better reproductions, her legs are smooth with no visible seams. I bought this product for my kids because of all the robot kits, this one specifically says "No soldering required" in the product feature. Well, that is not the truth. Soldering is required and it was quite disappointing.

My son and I have enjoyed the Scarab Robot Kit. The Product Features section on Amazon make it very clear that this is an intermediate soldering kit
We were able to build the robot in a day and it has worked perfectly.
We do have a couple of tips for those who are ready to give this project a try:
1) We would highly recommend getting a thin soldering tip for your soldering iron because YOU have to install and solder ALL the circuit board components, and they end up getting very close to each other. If you're using a Weller, get the st7 tip.
2) Pay close attention to the components that must be installed and soldered correctly or the robot will not work; the instructions were very clear about these components.
3) Once you've completed the circuit board, solder the capacitors and wires to the motors BEFORE installing the motors. This is out of step with the instructions, but it will make sense once you see how little space you will have to work with once the motors are installed.
4) The chassis and gear box uses tapping screws to hold it together; a thick handled 3mm phillips head screw driver is necessary to turn and tighten the screws effectively.

Last update: all in all, this cage was more crappy than good. Although it did take a beating, I think a lexan body could have handled just as much, so I don't really think there was a major advantage besides working well with my fpv system. On one of my last runs with it,managed to break off the welds on the frontount ( and it wasn't even a head on collision!!) I also noticed that some support beams were bent as well. In all honesty, it really wasn't that hard of a crash. After a few months of not very consistent use, it really shouldn't get mangled like that IMO...

I can no longer use this cage as I switched over to the LCG chassis, and the mounting holes on the cage don't line up at all. Oh well, I don't think that I will be missing it very much.

Update: the more I use this cage, the more I'm loving it. I have a really nice lexan body, but I seem to keep this one on 90% of the time ( mostly because it's a pain in the butt to take off and put back on). It is also perfect for my fpv setup, since the body does include some very convenient mounting holes for lights, etc. It was also very easy to fit a spare tire carrier that I made specifically for this cage.

Initially I was going to leave a scathing review, mainly because it seems like whoever welded this cage looked at at a slash once and then was like "ok let's do this" because the mounts just don't match up very well. However, the positives on this product do somewhat offset the negatives.

The mounting holes are ridiculously off, I had to really pull on the cage and chassis in order to make the bolts line up...it seems like it's putting a lot of stress on the chassis, so we'll see how it holds up in the long run.

This thing is HEAVY, after a few runs my shocks started squeaking really bad, I might have to replace them soon.

That being said, this thing takes a beating. I've crashed it, flipped it and accidentally rolled the car down a rocky embankment, and even though the paint got majorly scuffed up, the cage held up well... just make sure your antenna is inside the body, mine wasn't, and after the first roll, the cage sliced it right off.

Overall, I'm pretty satisfied with this cage. It will stay on my car as long as it holds up... however, I'm going to keep a close eye on the side mounting holes since they do seem to be getting stretched out due to the crappy mount hole placement of the cage

not as hard as this one was.

pieces and seems to really enjoy them and she feels a
true sense of accomplishment after each one.

We would recommend these as entertainment for anyone
with dementia or other mental challenges.

We bought this toy through Amazon during the 2013 holiday season and received it quickly. Without opening the toy, we wrapped it and gave it to our son on Christmas morning. He couldn't wait to play with it. I opened the sealed box and noted the toy was zip-tied onto the cardboard. Once I cut the toy free and turned it over, I noticed that there was no battery cover and the screw to lock it into place was also missing, rendering the toy useless. I immediately packaged it back up and saw that there was an email address on the box to contact the company, Casdon.

I quickly typed up an email describing the problem, attached a photo of the toy and the missing piece and sent it off. As of today I have yet to hear from them. I cannot find a local number for them and no other way of contacting them at all. I'm disgusted! Why put an email on a box if you aren't going to respond to customers?

Thankfully I bought this toy through Amazon and I'll be returning it through them as well. I'm disappointed because my son was so excited to play with the steering wheel but he won't be....there's no way I'll take a replacement. I'm done with anything this company puts out. Period.
